issue in the DLL proxy procedure [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that no handle could be found for any of the specified targets [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that the current DLL name could not be matched to any of the possible proxy DLL names v1.1.0.0: [-] Removed EfiGuard support as it conflicted with the Intel HV which caused a triple fault (forced system restart / CPU reset) -> You can still use EfiGuard, but you manually have to run "EfiDSEFix.exe -d" now in order to disable DSE and load the hypervisor [+] Added DLL proxy system for following DLLs: -> d3d9.dll d3d10.dll d3d11.dll d3d12.dll dinput8.dll dsound.dll version.dll wininet.dll winmm.dll winhttp.dll xinput_1_1.dll xinput_1_2.dll xinput_1_3.dll xinput_1_4.dll xinput9_1_0.dll xinputuap.dll [+] Added watchdog.exe-less approach, watchdog.exe will not be included in our releases anymore, all is done directly in the DLL now -> This utilizes Windows' RunDll32 program, which will ask for administrator permissions if AutoLoadHV is enabled [+] Added a XXH3 64-bit hash check for the protected Denuvo binary(ies) to let the user know that an incorrect game build/version is being used [+] Added a message box for when the HV crack is being run on an unsupported CPU type (e.g. old Intel Xeon CPUs, old AMD CPUs) [+] Replaced steamclient_loader_x64.exe with coldloader.dll, you must now start the game directly from the game EXE [+] Added a missing change from AMD HV to Intel HV, the Intel HV source code will be still labeled as V4 [#] Fixed an issue that caused "Steam offline" popup when the game folder path contained a dot Learn more here on HV releases: viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156419 viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156407 All questions related to issues & support should only be raised in the HV support topic: viewtopic.php?t=156435 Hypervisor-based releases are EXPERIMENTAL: It is possible to encounter BSODs, quite more likely on Intel processors atm.

releases.text: Crimson Desert (Hypervisor) (INTEL & AMD) - DenuvOwO Massive thanks to those who helped: 0xZeOn, Sagerao, Special For, Andreh, Mojtaba ❤️ Link: bzzhr.to link https://bzzhr.to/r3boj5u5o60l ⚠ Malicious ads - [READ ME] This site shows fake download pages with malware. Learn how to stay safe here . Game build is 23465675 Your CPU must support FMA3 and AVX. Make sure to read the DenuvOwO.nfo SHA-256: eb926dd5ea153f3f57eeb97a5f0f4f9f474055a5a64f826cf716dc16b882ac18 Code: Select all | Line number On/Off | Expand/Contract Changelog: v1.3.0.0: [+] The revert dialog now shows the exit code as a hexadecimal number [+] HV V6 release now spoofs NtSystemRoot (required for older Denuvo games) [-] Removed requirement of "Patches" section in INI [-] Removed requirement of "Sections" section in INI [-] Removed requirement of "Hashes" section in INI [#] Internal rework of the DLL/IAT spoofing code [#] Fixed NtContinueEx issue [#] Fixed some issues in the HV loading/detection code [#] Fixed detection of unsupported CPUs introduced in v1.1.0.0 (it never worked, until now) v1.2.0.1: [#] Internal fixes [#] Added missing source code file for Intel HV v1.2.0.0: [+] HV V5 update: -> Switched from CR3 to DR3 tracking [+] Updated DLL to support new DR3 system [+] Added new ntdll hooks for XState-related functions [#] Internal fixes and improvements v1.1.1.3: [#] Fixed an issue where the IAT spoofer spoofed IAT entries which pointed to a variable rather than to a function v1.1.1.2: [#] Fixed an issue where the IAT spoofer did not spoof proxied system DLLs (such as winmm.dll) v1.1.1.1: [#] Fixed an issue in the hypervisor detection system v1.1.1.0: [+] Added "LoadDlls" INI section, here you can define DLLs to load during DllMain sequence -> Format: <anything>=<DLLName> [-] Removed ColdLoader INI option v1.1.0.1: [+] Added more safeguards during the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the DLL proxy procedure [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that no handle could be found for any of the specified targets [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that the current DLL name could not be matched to any of the possible proxy DLL names v1.1.0.0: [-] Removed EfiGuard support as it conflicted with the Intel HV which caused a triple fault (forced system restart / CPU reset) -> You can still use EfiGuard, but you manually have to run "EfiDSEFix.exe -d" now in order to disable DSE and load the hypervisor [+] Added DLL proxy system for following DLLs: -> d3d9.dll d3d10.dll d3d11.dll d3d12.dll dinput8.dll dsound.dll version.dll wininet.dll winmm.dll winhttp.dll xinput_1_1.dll xinput_1_2.dll xinput_1_3.dll xinput_1_4.dll xinput9_1_0.dll xinputuap.dll [+] Added watchdog.exe-less approach, watchdog.exe will not be included in our releases anymore, all is done directly in the DLL now -> This utilizes Windows' RunDll32 program, which will ask for administrator permissions if AutoLoadHV is enabled [+] Added a XXH3 64-bit hash check for the protected Denuvo binary(ies) to let the user know that an incorrect game build/version is being used [+] Added a message box for when the HV crack is being run on an unsupported CPU type (e.g. old Intel Xeon CPUs, old AMD CPUs) [+] Replaced steamclient_loader_x64.exe with coldloader.dll, you must now start the game directly from the game EXE [+] Added a missing change from AMD HV to Intel HV, the Intel HV source code will be still labeled as V4 [#] Fixed an issue that caused "Steam offline" popup when the game folder path contained a dot Learn more here on HV releases: viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156419 viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156407 All questions related to issues & support should only be raised in the HV support topic: viewtopic.php?t=156435 Hypervisor-based releases are EXPERIMENTAL: It is possible to encounter BSODs, quite more likely on Intel processors atm.
cpuRequirements: Your CPU must support FMA3 and AVX

releases.text: Crimson Desert (Hypervisor) (INTEL & AMD) - DenuvOwO Massive thanks to those who helped: ZeOn, Sagerao, Special For, Andreh, Mojtaba ❤️ Link: vikingfile.com link https://vikingfile.com/f/ptr0OIYlUF ⚠ Malicious ads - [READ ME] This site shows fake download pages with malware. Learn how to stay safe here . Game build is 23193536 Your CPU must support FMA3 and AVX. Make sure to read the DenuvOwO.nfo SHA-256: 568429fbf4d0e5fd99e91e399c3bee3a7812700e7b047b32bbdba0a80662724c Code: Select all | Line number On/Off | Expand/Contract Changelog: v1.3.0.0: [+] The revert dialog now shows the exit code as a hexadecimal number [+] HV V6 release now spoofs NtSystemRoot (required for older Denuvo games) [-] Removed requirement of "Patches" section in INI [-] Removed requirement of "Sections" section in INI [-] Removed requirement of "Hashes" section in INI [#] Internal rework of the DLL/IAT spoofing code [#] Fixed NtContinueEx issue [#] Fixed some issues in the HV loading/detection code [#] Fixed detection of unsupported CPUs introduced in v1.1.0.0 (it never worked, until now) v1.2.0.1: [#] Internal fixes [#] Added missing source code file for Intel HV v1.2.0.0: [+] HV V5 update: -> Switched from CR3 to DR3 tracking [+] Updated DLL to support new DR3 system [+] Added new ntdll hooks for XState-related functions [#] Internal fixes and improvements v1.1.1.3: [#] Fixed an issue where the IAT spoofer spoofed IAT entries which pointed to a variable rather than to a function v1.1.1.2: [#] Fixed an issue where the IAT spoofer did not spoof proxied system DLLs (such as winmm.dll) v1.1.1.1: [#] Fixed an issue in the hypervisor detection system v1.1.1.0: [+] Added "LoadDlls" INI section, here you can define DLLs to load during DllMain sequence -> Format: <anything>=<DLLName> [-] Removed ColdLoader INI option v1.1.0.1: [+] Added more safeguards during the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the DLL proxy procedure [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that no handle could be found for any of the specified targets [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that the current DLL name could not be matched to any of the possible proxy DLL names v1.1.0.0: [-] Removed EfiGuard support as it conflicted with the Intel HV which caused a triple fault (forced system restart / CPU reset) -> You can still use EfiGuard, but you manually have to run "EfiDSEFix.exe -d" now in order to disable DSE and load the hypervisor [+] Added DLL proxy system for following DLLs: -> d3d9.dll d3d10.dll d3d11.dll d3d12.dll dinput8.dll dsound.dll version.dll wininet.dll winmm.dll winhttp.dll xinput_1_1.dll xinput_1_2.dll xinput_1_3.dll xinput_1_4.dll xinput9_1_0.dll xinputuap.dll [+] Added watchdog.exe-less approach, watchdog.exe will not be included in our releases anymore, all is done directly in the DLL now -> This utilizes Windows' RunDll32 program, which will ask for administrator permissions if AutoLoadHV is enabled [+] Added a XXH3 64-bit hash check for the protected Denuvo binary(ies) to let the user know that an incorrect game build/version is being used [+] Added a message box for when the HV crack is being run on an unsupported CPU type (e.g. old Intel Xeon CPUs, old AMD CPUs) [+] Replaced steamclient_loader_x64.exe with coldloader.dll, you must now start the game directly from the game EXE [+] Added a missing change from AMD HV to Intel HV, the Intel HV source code will be still labeled as V4 [#] Fixed an issue that caused "Steam offline" popup when the game folder path contained a dot Learn more here on HV releases: viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156419 viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156407 All questions related to issues & support should only be raised in the HV support topic: viewtopic.php?t=156435 Hypervisor-based releases are EXPERIMENTAL: It is possible to encounter BSODs, quite more likely on Intel processors atm.
cpuRequirements: Your CPU must support FMA3 and AVX

v1.3.0.0: [+] The revert dialog now shows the exit code as a hexadecimal number [+] HV V6 release now spoofs NtSystemRoot (required for older Denuvo games) [-] Removed requirement of "Patches" section in INI [-] Removed requirement of "Sections" section in INI [-] Removed requirement of "Hashes" section in INI [#] Internal rework of the DLL/IAT spoofing code [#] Fixed NtContinueEx issue [#] Fixed some issues in the HV loading/detection code [#] Fixed detection of unsupported CPUs introduced in v1.1.0.0 (it never worked, until now) v1.2.0.1: [#] Internal fixes [#] Added missing source code file for Intel HV v1.2.0.0: [+] HV V5 update: -> Switched from CR3 to DR3 tracking [+] Updated DLL to support new DR3 system [+] Added new ntdll hooks for XState-related functions [#] Internal fixes and improvements v1.1.1.3: [#] Fixed an issue where the IAT spoofer spoofed IAT entries which pointed to a variable rather than to a function v1.1.1.2: [#] Fixed an issue where the IAT spoofer did not spoof proxied system DLLs (such as winmm.dll) v1.1.1.1: [#] Fixed an issue in the hypervisor detection system v1.1.1.0: [+] Added "LoadDlls" INI section, here you can define DLLs to load during DllMain sequence -> Format: <anything>=<DLLName> [-] Removed ColdLoader INI option v1.1.0.1: [+] Added more safeguards during the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the DLL proxy procedure [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that no handle could be found for any of the specified targets [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that the current DLL name could not be matched to any of the possible proxy DLL names v1.1.0.0: [-] Removed EfiGuard support as it conflicted with the Intel HV which caused a triple fault (forced system restart / CPU reset) -> You can still use EfiGuard, but you manually have to run "EfiDSEFix.exe -d" now in order to disable DSE and load the hypervisor [+] Added DLL proxy system for following DLLs: -> d3d9.dll d3d10.dll d3d11.dll d3d12.dll dinput8.dll dsound.dll version.dll wininet.dll winmm.dll winhttp.dll xinput_1_1.dll xinput_1_2.dll xinput_1_3.dll xinput_1_4.dll xinput9_1_0.dll xinputuap.dll [+] Added watchdog.exe-less approach, watchdog.exe will not be included in our releases anymore, all is done directly in the DLL now -> This utilizes Windows' RunDll32 program, which will ask for administrator permissions if AutoLoadHV is enabled [+] Added a XXH3 64-bit hash check for the protected Denuvo binary(ies) to let the user know that an incorrect game build/version is being used [+] Added a message box for when the HV crack is being run on an unsupported CPU type (e.g. old Intel Xeon CPUs, old AMD CPUs) [+] Replaced steamclient_loader_x64.exe with coldloader.dll, you must now start the game directly from the game EXE [+] Added a missing change from AMD HV to Intel HV, the Intel HV source code will be still labeled as V4 [#] Fixed an issue that caused "Steam offline" popup when the game folder path contained a dot Learn more here on HV releases: viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156419 viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156407 All questions related to issues & support should only be raised in the HV support topic: viewtopic.php?t=156435 Hypervisor-based releases are EXPERIMENTAL: It is possible to encounter BSODs, quite more likely on Intel processors atm.

Make sure to read the DenuvOwO.nfo SHA-256: 9b5d7142614799fe04280899f075286e2b492e96758ed56c1b9f27cbd8159a5d Code: Select all | Line number On/Off | Expand/Contract Changelog: v1.2.0.1: [#] Internal fixes [#] Added missing source code file for Intel HV v1.2.0.0: [+] HV V5 update: -> Switched from CR3 to DR3 tracking [+] Updated DLL to support new DR3 system [+] Added new ntdll hooks for XState-related functions [#] Internal fixes and improvements v1.1.1.3: [#] Fixed an issue where the IAT spoofer spoofed IAT entries which pointed to a variable rather than to a function v1.1.1.2: [#] Fixed an issue where the IAT spoofer did not spoof proxied system DLLs (such as winmm.dll) v1.1.1.1: [#] Fixed an issue in the hypervisor detection system v1.1.1.0: [+] Added "LoadDlls" INI section, here you can define DLLs to load during DllMain sequence -> Format: <anything>=<DLLName> [-] Removed ColdLoader INI option v1.1.0.1: [+] Added more safeguards during the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the DLL proxy procedure [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that no handle could be found for any of the specified targets [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that the current DLL name could not be matched to any of the possible proxy DLL names v1.1.0.0: [-] Removed EfiGuard support as it conflicted with the Intel HV which caused a triple fault (forced system restart / CPU reset) -> You can still use EfiGuard, but you manually have to run "EfiDSEFix.exe -d" now in order to disable DSE and load the hypervisor [+] Added DLL proxy system for following DLLs: -> d3d9.dll d3d10.dll d3d11.dll d3d12.dll dinput8.dll dsound.dll version.dll wininet.dll winmm.dll winhttp.dll xinput_1_1.dll xinput_1_2.dll xinput_1_3.dll xinput_1_4.dll xinput9_1_0.dll xinputuap.dll [+] Added watchdog.exe-less approach, watchdog.exe will not be included in our releases anymore, all is done directly in the DLL now -> This utilizes Windows' RunDll32 program, which will ask for administrator permissions if AutoLoadHV is enabled [+] Added a XXH3 64-bit hash check for the protected Denuvo binary(ies) to let the user know that an incorrect game build/version is being used [+] Added a message box for when the HV crack is being run on an unsupported CPU type (e.g. old Intel Xeon CPUs, old AMD CPUs) [+] Replaced steamclient_loader_x64.exe with coldloader.dll, you must now start the game directly from the game EXE [+] Added a missing change from AMD HV to Intel HV, the Intel HV source code will be still labeled as V4 [#] Fixed an issue that caused "Steam offline" popup when the game folder path contained a dot Learn more here on HV releases: viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156419 Game build is 22748572 Make sure to read the DenuvOwO.nfo SHA-256: e9ebbcada9e4f3bdc057bcd07420091824227cdb266803cd891f03fc5026ebdf Code: Select all | Line number On/Off | Expand/Contract Changelog: v1.2.0.0: [+] HV V5 update: -> Switched from CR3 to DR3 tracking [+] Updated DLL to support new DR3 system [+] Added new ntdll hooks for XState-related functions [#] Internal fixes and improvements v1.1.1.3: [#] Fixed an issue where the IAT spoofer spoofed IAT entries which pointed to a variable rather than to a function v1.1.1.2: [#] Fixed an issue where the IAT spoofer did not spoof proxied system DLLs (such as winmm.dll) v1.1.1.1: [#] Fixed an issue in the hypervisor detection system v1.1.1.0: [+] Added "LoadDlls" INI section, here you can define DLLs to load during DllMain sequence -> Format: <anything>=<DLLName> [-] Removed ColdLoader INI option v1.1.0.1: [+] Added more safeguards during the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the DLL proxy procedure [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that no handle could be found for any of the specified targets [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that the current DLL name could not be matched to any of the possible proxy DLL names v1.1.0.0: [-] Removed EfiGuard support as it conflicted with the Intel HV which caused a triple fault (forced system restart / CPU reset) -> You can still use EfiGuard, but you manually have to run "EfiDSEFix.exe -d" now in order to disable DSE and load the hypervisor [+] Added DLL proxy system for following DLLs: -> d3d9.dll d3d10.dll d3d11.dll d3d12.dll dinput8.dll dsound.dll version.dll wininet.dll winmm.dll winhttp.dll xinput_1_1.dll xinput_1_2.dll xinput_1_3.dll xinput_1_4.dll xinput9_1_0.dll xinputuap.dll [+] Added watchdog.exe-less approach, watchdog.exe will not be included in our releases anymore, all is done directly in the DLL now -> This utilizes Windows' RunDll32 program, which will ask for administrator permissions if AutoLoadHV is enabled [+] Added a XXH3 64-bit hash check for the protected Denuvo binary(ies) to let the user know that an incorrect game build/version is being used [+] Added a message box for when the HV crack is being run on an unsupported CPU type (e.g. old Intel Xeon CPUs, old AMD CPUs) [+] Replaced steamclient_loader_x64.exe with coldloader.dll, you must now start the game directly from the game EXE [+] Added a missing change from AMD HV to Intel HV, the Intel HV source code will be still labeled as V4 [#] Fixed an issue that caused "Steam offline" popup when the game folder path contained a dot Learn more here on HV releases: viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156419 viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156407 All questions related to issues & support should only be raised in the HV support topic: viewtopic.php?t=156435 Hypervisor-based releases are EXPERIMENTAL: It is possible to encounter BSODs, quite more likely on Intel processors atm.

Line number On/Off | Expand/Contract Changelog: v1.1.1.0: [+] Added "LoadDlls" INI section, here you can define DLLs to load during DllMain sequence -> Format: <anything>=<DLLName> [-] Removed ColdLoader INI option v1.1.0.1: [+] Added more safeguards during the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the DLL proxy procedure [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that no handle could be found for any of the specified targets [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that the current DLL name could not be matched to any of the possible proxy DLL names v1.1.0.0: [-] Removed EfiGuard support as it conflicted with the Intel HV which caused a triple fault (forced system restart / CPU reset) -> You can still use EfiGuard, but you manually have to run "EfiDSEFix.exe -d" now in order to disable DSE and load the hypervisor [+] Added DLL proxy system for following DLLs: -> d3d9.dll d3d10.dll d3d11.dll d3d12.dll dinput8.dll dsound.dll version.dll wininet.dll winmm.dll winhttp.dll xinput_1_1.dll xinput_1_2.dll xinput_1_3.dll xinput_1_4.dll xinput9_1_0.dll xinputuap.dll [+] Added watchdog.exe-less approach, watchdog.exe will not be included in our releases anymore, all is done directly in the DLL now -> This utilizes Windows' RunDll32 program, which will ask for administrator permissions if AutoLoadHV is enabled [+] Added a XXH3 64-bit hash check for the protected Denuvo binary(ies) to let the user know that an incorrect game build/version is being used [+] Added a message box for when the HV crack is being run on an unsupported CPU type (e.g. old Intel Xeon CPUs, old AMD CPUs) [+] Replaced steamclient_loader_x64.exe with coldloader.dll, you must now start the game directly from the game EXE [+] Added a missing change from AMD HV to Intel HV, the Intel HV source code will be still labeled as V4 [#] Fixed an issue that caused "Steam offline" popup when the game folder path releases.author: DenuvOwO
releases.text: Crimson Desert (Hypervisor) (INTEL & AMD) - DenuvOwO Massive thanks to those who helped: ZeOn, Sagerao, Special For, Andreh, Mojtaba ❤️ Link: vikingfile.com link https://vikingfile.com/f/3cl4uanwww ⚠ Malicious ads - [READ ME] This site shows fake download pages with malware. Learn how to stay safe here . Game build is 22643803 Make sure to read the DenuvOwO.nfo SHA-256: a82f3a29731864bab582cf087f9964843751c6d0e1aec7cc20a6e8d063d1af8f Code: Select all | Line number On/Off | Expand/Contract Changelog: v1.1.1.2: [#] Fixed an issue where the IAT spoofer did not spoof proxied system DLLs (such as winmm.dll) v1.1.1.1: [#] Fixed an issue in the hypervisor detection system v1.1.1.0: [+] Added "LoadDlls" INI section, here you can define DLLs to load during DllMain sequence -> Format: <anything>=<DLLName> [-] Removed ColdLoader INI option v1.1.0.1: [+] Added more safeguards during the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the DLL proxy procedure [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that no handle could be found for any of the specified targets [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that the current DLL name could not be matched to any of the possible proxy DLL names v1.1.0.0: [-] Removed EfiGuard support as it conflicted with the Intel HV which caused a triple fault (forced system restart / CPU reset) -> You can still use EfiGuard, but you manually have to run "EfiDSEFix.exe -d" now in order to disable DSE and load the hypervisor [+] Added DLL proxy system for following DLLs: -> d3d9.dll d3d10.dll d3d11.dll d3d12.dll dinput8.dll dsound.dll version.dll wininet.dll winmm.dll winhttp.dll xinput_1_1.dll xinput_1_2.dll xinput_1_3.dll xinput_1_4.dll xinput9_1_0.dll xinputuap.dll [+] Added watchdog.exe-less approach, watchdog.exe will not be included in our releases anymore, all is done directly in the DLL now -> This utilizes Windows' RunDll32 program, which will ask for administrator permissions if AutoLoadHV is enabled [+] Added a XXH3 64-bit hash check for the protected Denuvo binary(ies) to let the user know that an incorrect game build/version is being used [+] Added a message box for when the HV crack is being run on an unsupported CPU type (e.g. old Intel Xeon CPUs, old AMD CPUs) [+] Replaced steamclient_loader_x64.exe with coldloader.dll, you must now start the game directly from the game EXE [+] Added a missing change from AMD HV to Intel HV, the Intel HV source code will be still labeled as V4 [#] Fixed an issue that caused "Steam offline" popup when the game folder path contained a dot Learn more here on HV releases: viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156419 viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156407 All questions related to issues & support should only be raised in the HV support topic: viewtopic.php?t=156435 Hypervisor-based releases are EXPERIMENTAL: It is possible to encounter BSODs, quite more likely on Intel processors atm.

releases.text: EA SPORTS™ Madden NFL 26 (Hypervisor) (INTEL & AMD) - DenuvOwO Massive thanks to those who helped: ZeOn, Sagerao, Special For, Andreh, Mojtaba ❤️ Link (CrackFix): vikingfile.com link https://vikingfile.com/f/l1Nbjdi83S ⚠ Malicious ads - [READ ME] This site shows fake download pages with malware. Learn how to stay safe here . Game version is 1.0.133.17820 Make sure to read the DenuvOwO.nfo SHA-256: 3f897fe60e442ce135885a921a2fd8367b290dc2f2962fabf2edc32c63f3414 Code: Select all | Line number On/Off | Expand/Contract Changelog: v1.1.1.0: [+] Added "LoadDlls" INI section, here you can define DLLs to load during DllMain sequence -> Format: <anything>=<DLLName> [-] Removed ColdLoader INI option v1.1.0.1: [+] Added more safeguards during the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the DLL proxy procedure [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that no handle could be found for any of the specified targets [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that the current DLL name could not be matched to any of the possible proxy DLL names v1.1.0.0: [-] Removed EfiGuard support as it conflicted with the Intel HV which caused a triple fault (forced system restart / CPU reset) -> You can still use EfiGuard, but you manually have to run "EfiDSEFix.exe -d" now in order to disable DSE and load the hypervisor [+] Added DLL proxy system for following DLLs: -> d3d9.dll d3d10.dll d3d11.dll d3d12.dll dinput8.dll dsound.dll version.dll wininet.dll winmm.dll winhttp.dll xinput_1_1.dll xinput_1_2.dll xinput_1_3.dll xinput_1_4.dll xinput9_1_0.dll xinputuap.dll [+] Added watchdog.exe-less approach, watchdog.exe will not be included in our releases anymore, all is done directly in the DLL now -> This utilizes Windows' RunDll32 program, which will ask for administrator permissions if AutoLoadHV is enabled [+] Added a XXH3 64-bit hash check for the protected Denuvo binary(ies) to let the user know that an incorrect game build/version is being used [+] Added a message box for when the HV crack is being run on an unsupported CPU type (e.g. old Intel Xeon CPUs, old AMD CPUs) [+] Replaced steamclient_loader_x64.exe with coldloader.dll, you must now start the game directly from the game EXE [+] Added a missing change from AMD HV to Intel HV, the Intel HV source code will be still labeled as V4 [#] Fixed an issue that caused "Steam offline" popup when the game folder path contained a dot Learn more here on HV releases: viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156419 viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156407 All questions related to issues & support should only be raised in the HV support topic: viewtopic.php?t=156435 Hypervisor-based releases are EXPERIMENTAL: It is possible to encounter BSODs, quite more likely on Intel processors atm.

Game build is 17356935 Make sure to read the DenuvOwO.nfo SHA-256: 6911afaf27563ea179088d8009656b207419eeca185e00e491ca5ac25de10ce7 Code: Select all | Line number On/Off | Expand/Contract Changelog: v1.1.1.0: [+] Added "LoadDlls" INI section, here you can define DLLs to load during DllMain sequence -> Format: <anything>=<DLLName> [-] Removed ColdLoader INI option v1.1.0.1: [+] Added more safeguards during the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the DLL proxy procedure [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that no handle could be found for any of the specified targets [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that the current DLL name could not be matched to any of the possible proxy DLL names v1.1.0.0: [-] Removed EfiGuard support as it conflicted with the Intel HV which caused a triple fault (forced system restart / CPU reset) -> You can still use EfiGuard, but you manually have to run "EfiDSEFix.exe -d" now in order to disable DSE and load the hypervisor [+] Added DLL proxy system for following DLLs: -> d3d9.dll d3d10.dll d3d11.dll d3d12.dll dinput8.dll dsound.dll version.dll wininet.dll winmm.dll winhttp.dll xinput_1_1.dll xinput_1_2.dll xinput_1_3.dll xinput_1_4.dll xinput9_1_0.dll xinputuap.dll [+] Added watchdog.exe-less approach, watchdog.exe will not be included in our releases anymore, all is done directly in the DLL now -> This utilizes Windows' RunDll32 program, which will ask for administrator permissions if AutoLoadHV is enabled [+] Added a XXH3 64-bit hash check for the protected Denuvo binary(ies) to let the user know that an incorrect game build/version is being used [+] Added a message box for when the HV crack is being run on an unsupported CPU type (e.g. old Intel Xeon CPUs, old AMD CPUs) [+] Replaced steamclient_loader_x64.exe with coldloader.dll, you must now start the game directly from the game EXE [+] Added a missing change from AMD HV to Intel HV, the Intel HV source code will be still labeled as V4 [#] Fixed an issue that caused "Steam offline" popup when the game folder path contained a dot Learn more here on HV releases: viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156419 viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156407 All questions related to issues & support should only be raised in the HV support topic: viewtopic.php?t=156435 Hypervisor-based releases are EXPERIMENTAL: It is possible to encounter BSODs, quite more likely on Intel processors atm.

Changelog: v1.1.0.1: [+] Added more safeguards during the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the HV load/unload procedure [#] Fixed an issue in the DLL proxy procedure [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that no handle could be found for any of the specified targets [-] Removed the error message box that tells you that the current DLL name could not be matched to any of the possible proxy DLL names v1.1.0.0: [-] Removed EfiGuard support as it conflicted with the Intel HV which caused a triple fault (forced system restart / CPU reset) -> You can still use EfiGuard, but you manually have to run "EfiDSEFix.exe -d" now in order to disable DSE and load the hypervisor [+] Added DLL proxy system for following DLLs: -> d3d9.dll d3d10.dll d3d11.dll d3d12.dll dinput8.dll dsound.dll version.dll wininet.dll winmm.dll winhttp.dll xinput_1_1.dll xinput_1_2.dll xinput_1_3.dll xinput_1_4.dll xinput9_1_0.dll xinputuap.dll [+] Added watchdog.exe-less approach, watchdog.exe will not be included in our releases anymore, all is done directly in the DLL now -> This utilizes Windows' RunDll32 program, which will ask for administrator permissions if AutoLoadHV is enabled [+] Added a XXH3 64-bit hash check for the protected Denuvo binary(ies) to let the user know that an incorrect game build/version is being used [+] Added a message box for when the HV crack is being run on an unsupported CPU type (e.g. old Intel Xeon CPUs, old AMD CPUs) [+] Replaced steamclient_loader_x64.exe with coldloader.dll, you must now start the game directly from the game EXE [+] Added a missing change from AMD HV to Intel HV, the Intel HV source code will be still labeled as V4 [#] Fixed an issue that caused "Steam offline" popup when the game folder path contained a dot Learn more here on HV releases: viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156419 viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156407 All questions related to issues & support should only be raised in the HV support topic: viewtopic.php?t=156435 Hypervisor-based releases are EXPERIMENTAL: It is possible to encounter BSODs, quite more likely on Intel processors atm.

Game build is 22565118 SHA-256: bbc89e2f7d91be7167069cabc67a907456885d3b88fe3101c6cc6f2e70479a52 Make sure to read the DenuvOwO.nfo Code: Select all | Line number On/Off | Expand/Contract Changelog: v1.1.0.0: [-] Removed EfiGuard support as it conflicted with the Intel HV which caused a triple fault (forced system restart / CPU reset) -> You can still use EfiGuard, but you manually have to run "EfiDSEFix.exe -d" now in order to disable DSE and load the hypervisor [+] Added DLL proxy system for following DLLs: -> d3d9.dll d3d10.dll d3d11.dll d3d12.dll dinput8.dll dsound.dll version.dll wininet.dll winmm.dll winhttp.dll xinput_1_1.dll xinput_1_2.dll xinput_1_3.dll xinput_1_4.dll xinput9_1_0.dll xinputuap.dll [+] Added watchdog.exe-less approach, watchdog.exe will not be included in our releases anymore, all is done directly in the DLL now -> This utilizes Windows' RunDll32 program, which will ask for administrator permissions if AutoLoadHV is enabled [+] Added a XXH3 64-bit hash check for the protected Denuvo binary(ies) to let the user know that an incorrect game build/version is being used [+] Added a message box for when the HV crack is being run on an unsupported CPU type (e.g. old Intel Xeon CPUs, old AMD CPUs) [+] Replaced steamclient_loader_x64.exe with coldloader.dll, you must now start the game directly from the game EXE [+] Added a missing change from AMD HV to Intel HV, the Intel HV source code will be still labeled as V4 [#] Fixed an issue that caused "Steam offline" popup when the game folder path contained a dot Learn more here on HV releases: viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156419 viewtopic.php?f=14&t=156407 All questions related to issues & support should only be raised in the HV support topic: viewtopic.php?t=156435 Hypervisor-based releases are EXPERIMENTAL: It is possible to encounter BSODs, quite more likely on Intel processors atm.
